questions.question: Describe Griffin’s discovery of invisibility.
questions.answer: Griffin was a brilliant scientist who discovered a method to make the human body invisible through scientific experiments. He swallowed rare drugs and carried out experiments that made his body transparent like glass. This discovery made him powerful but also isolated.

questions.question: Why did Griffin set fire to his landlord’s house?
questions.answer: Griffin set fire to his landlord’s house out of anger and revenge. His landlord demanded rent and tried to interfere with his experiments, which enraged Griffin and led him to destroy the house.

questions.question: Explain why winter was the worst season for Griffin.
questions.answer: Winter was the worst season for Griffin because he could not wear clothes to protect himself from the cold. Since clothes did not become invisible, he had to move around naked, which made survival very difficult.

questions.question: Describe the incident of the footprints without feet.
questions.answer: People saw footprints appearing on the ground without seeing any person. These footprints were created because Griffin’s feet were muddy, even though his body was invisible. This mysterious incident created fear among the public.

questions.question: How did Griffin manage to survive in the departmental store?
questions.answer: Griffin hid in a departmental store at night, stole food and clothes, and slept there. However, he made the mistake of sleeping too long and was discovered when the store opened.

questions.question: Why did Griffin go to the village of Iping?
questions.answer: Griffin went to Iping to live in solitude and hide his invisibility. He wanted a quiet place where he could continue his experiments without attracting attention.

questions.question: Describe Mrs Hall’s attitude towards Griffin.
questions.answer: Mrs Hall initially treated Griffin politely because he paid rent in advance. She believed he was an eccentric scientist, but later became suspicious of his strange behaviour.

questions.question: What strange events occurred at the inn?
questions.answer: At the inn, furniture moved on its own, doors opened and closed mysteriously, and objects appeared to be handled by invisible hands. These events frightened everyone.

questions.question: Why did villagers begin to suspect Griffin?
questions.answer: Villagers suspected Griffin because of his strange behaviour and because he suddenly had money after claiming to be poor earlier.

questions.question: Describe the scene when Griffin reveals his invisibility.
questions.answer: When questioned, Griffin removed his bandages and clothes, revealing a headless man. This shocking sight terrified the people present.

questions.question: Why was Mr Jaffers unable to arrest Griffin?
questions.answer: Mr Jaffers could not arrest Griffin because Griffin became completely invisible and escaped easily.

questions.question: Explain Griffin’s character in brief.
questions.answer: Griffin is intelligent but selfish and lawless. He misuses science for personal gain and lacks moral responsibility.

questions.question: What genre does the story belong to? Explain.
questions.answer: The story belongs to the science fiction genre because it deals with imaginary scientific discoveries like invisibility.

questions.question: Explain how fear dominates the villagers’ behaviour.
questions.answer: Fear of the unknown makes the villagers suspicious and aggressive. They panic instead of thinking rationally.

questions.question: Why does Griffin fail despite being intelligent?
questions.answer: Griffin fails because he lacks moral values and misuses his intelligence.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the story?
questions.answer: The central theme is that scientific knowledge must be guided by ethics and responsibility.

questions.question: Explain the title ‘Footprints Without Feet’.
questions.answer: The title refers to the mysterious footprints left by an invisible man, highlighting the central mystery of the story.

questions.question: How does invisibility isolate Griffin?
questions.answer: Invisibility separates Griffin from normal human interaction and makes him lonely.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches that power without ethics leads to destruction.

questions.question: Why does Griffin become a threat to society?
questions.answer: Griffin becomes a threat because he misuses his scientific power and commits crimes.

questions.question: How does the story show misuse of science?
questions.answer: The story shows misuse of science through Griffin’s criminal actions and lack of responsibility.

questions.question: Explain why Griffin is called lawless.
questions.answer: Griffin breaks laws by stealing, attacking people, and creating fear.

questions.question: What role does mystery play in the story?
questions.answer: Mystery creates suspense and fear, keeping readers engaged.

questions.question: How does the story reflect human weaknesses?
questions.answer: It reflects greed, arrogance, and fear in human behaviour.

questions.question: Why is ‘Footprints Without Feet’ still relevant today?
questions.answer: The story is relevant today because it warns against unethical use of modern science.
